verses.1: A man who isolates himself pursues selfishness,
verses.2: A fool has no delight in understanding,
verses.3: When wickedness comes, contempt also comes,
verses.4: The words of a man’s mouth are like deep waters.
verses.5: To be partial to the faces of the wicked is not good,
verses.6: A fool’s lips come into strife,
verses.7: A fool’s mouth is his destruction,
verses.8: The words of a gossip are like dainty morsels:
verses.9: One who is slack in his work
verses.10: The LORD’s name is a strong tower:
verses.11: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city,
verses.12: Before destruction the heart of man is proud,
verses.13: He who answers before he hears,
verses.14: A man’s spirit will sustain him in sickness,
verses.15: The heart of the discerning gets knowledge.
verses.16: A man’s gift makes room for him,
verses.17: He who pleads his cause first seems right—
verses.19: A brother offended is more difficult than a fortified city.
verses.20: A man’s stomach is filled with the fruit of his mouth.
verses.21: Death and life are in the power of the tongue;
verses.22: Whoever finds a wife finds a good thing,
verses.24: A man of many companions may be ruined,
